LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT SBC INTERNET SERVICES MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. MATTER WAS ARGUED AND THE COURT DENIED THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT; AND DENIED THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION SOUGHT IN MOTION (2ND-NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, 5TH-INVASION OF PRIVACY AND 6TH-NEGLIGENCE) WITH THE EXCEPTION THAT THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION AS TO THE FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION (3RD-INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION) -- SUBMITTED ON OCT-26-2007 BY JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER FOR THE COURT'S CONSIDERATION, TO INCLUDE IN THE ALTERNATIVE RE THE FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION, DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT DID NOT SHIFT BURDEN OR GRANTED BECAUSE DEFENDANT SHIFTED BURDEN. PROPOSED ORDER TO ALSO COMPLY WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, REPORTER: JANET POND, CSR #5292

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Nov-06-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SBC INTERNET SERVICES Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - OFF CALENDAR PER OCTOBER 2, 2007 REQUEST OF MOVING PARTY. (302/PJB) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-13-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SBC INTERNET SERVICES MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Terence O. Mayo, Esq. of Mayo & Rogers 397-1515 for pltfs & opposing parties; Raymond P. Bolanos, Esq. 772-1357 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court denied the motion for summary judgment; and denied the motion for summary adjudication as to all causes of action sought in motion (2nd-negligent infliction of emotional distress, 5th-invasion of privacy and 6th-negligence) with the exception that the Court took the matter under submission as to the fraud cause of action (3rd-intentional misrepresentation). Plaintiff to prepare proposed order for the Court's consideration, to include in the alternative re the fraud cause of action, denied because defendant did not shift burden or granted because defendant shifted burden. Proposed order to also comply with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312. (302/REQ/AY) JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, REPORTER: JANET POND, CSR #5292
